fields.summary: Service AM support for decommissioning component instances

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: The idea behind this feature is to have a flex down where specific component instances are removed. Currently on a flex down, the service AM chooses for removal the component instances with the highest IDs.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Allow services to release/kill specific containers

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

fields.summary: Service AM support for restoring decommissioned component instances

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

comments.body: I think to allow removing specific component instances, we will need to maintain a list of decommissioned instances in the Component spec for the service. This will prevent future AM attempts from assigning containers to the decommissioned instances. We should be able to support decommissioning by component instance name or by instance hostname (componentInstanceName.serviceName.user.domain).
Patch 01 is a first draft of a decommissioning capability in the service AM. I still need to do more testing of this feature.

This feature will allow decommission of containers.  The hostname sequence number will not be linear after one of the instances is decommissioned.  We may need to build a restore container feature to keep YARN service hostname linear.  Some applications may assume that hostnames are always linear or equal to number of containers to spawn for the service.  This patch will change the undocumented behavior.  Other than that patch 05 looks good to me.

[~eyang] [~leftnoteasy] [~sunilg] I'm interested in backporting this patch to branch-3.2 and branch-3.1. This patch will be useful for platforms such as OpenWhisk that have the ability to remove specific containers when scaling down.

I'm not sure I agree that this is an incompatible change. For apps that require the component instance numbers to be linearly increasing, users can avoid using the decommission capability and instead can use the original flex capability for removing containers.

[~billie.rinaldi] There is a schematic change in the way container can be decommissioned.  While this schematic change is marginal, it still has potential risk to let user stumble on hostname gaps that did not exist in branch-3.1 and branch-3.2 before.  I don't have a opinion not to back port this, the flag is to let customer to know the change of behavior.  It may be insignificant that most users don't care.
The patch is a clean cherry-pick to branch-3.1, but I am attaching the resulting patch for precommit build to be safe.
+1 to back port to branch-3.1, branch-3.2, thanks [~billie.rinaldi], [~eyang].

comments.body: I think to allow removing specific component instances, we will need to maintain a list of decommissioned instances in the Component spec for the service. This will prevent future AM attempts from assigning containers to the decommissioned instances. We should be able to support decommissioning by component instance name or by instance hostname (componentInstanceName.serviceName.user.domain).
Patch 01 is a first draft of a decommissioning capability in the service AM. I still need to do more testing of this feature.

This feature will allow decommission of containers.  The hostname sequence number will not be linear after one of the instances is decommissioned.  We may need to build a restore container feature to keep YARN service hostname linear.  Some applications may assume that hostnames are always linear or equal to number of containers to spawn for the service.  This patch will change the undocumented behavior.  Other than that patch 05 looks good to me.

+1 committing shortly.

[~eyang] [~leftnoteasy] [~sunilg] I'm interested in backporting this patch to branch-3.2 and branch-3.1. This patch will be useful for platforms such as OpenWhisk that have the ability to remove specific containers when scaling down.

I'm not sure I agree that this is an incompatible change. For apps that require the component instance numbers to be linearly increasing, users can avoid using the decommission capability and instead can use the original flex capability for removing containers.

[~billie.rinaldi] There is a schematic change in the way container can be decommissioned.  While this schematic change is marginal, it still has potential risk to let user stumble on hostname gaps that did not exist in branch-3.1 and branch-3.2 before.  I don't have a opinion not to back port this, the flag is to let customer to know the change of behavior.  It may be insignificant that most users don't care.
The patch is a clean cherry-pick to branch-3.1, but I am attaching the resulting patch for precommit build to be safe.
+1 to back port to branch-3.1, branch-3.2, thanks [~billie.rinaldi], [~eyang].
